message:
fix(freeze): Fix CV reference handling and nullish equality

Two major fixes for frozen code correctness:

1. CV.eq now implements JavaScript loose equality (==) for null/undefined:
   - null == undefined returns true
   - undefined == null returns true
   This fixes "cannot read property 'X' of undefined" errors where code
   checks (node == null) but node is undefined.

2. Remove incorrect JSValue.free() calls after CV.toJSValue():
   - CV.toJSValue() reconstructs JSValue from compressed bits WITHOUT duping
   - Calling free() after toJSValue() decrements refcount of live objects
   - Fixed in: get_field, put_field, get_length, instanceof, delete,
     define_array_el, iterator_close, iterator_get_value_done
   - Also fixed type errors in put_var_ref opcodes (was calling JSValue.free
     on CV type)

These fixes address use-after-free bugs in frozen TypeScript compiler code.
